heading: Advanced systems program implementation by Secretary of Energy
label: 2703 Advanced systems program implementation by Secretary of Energy
notes: References in TextThis Act, referred to in subsec. (f), is , , , known as the Department of Energy Act of 1978—Civilian Applications. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables.Pub. L. 95–23892 Stat. 47Feb. 25, 1978
Amendments1994—Subsec. (f)(3). substituted “Committee on Science, Space, and Technology” for “Committee on Science and Technology”.Pub. L. 103–437

Amendments1994—Subsec. (f)(3). substituted “Committee on Science, Space, and Technology” for “Committee on Science and Technology”.Pub. L. 103–437

children.heading: Establishment and conduct of program
children.label: a Establishment and conduct of program

children.text: establish and conduct new projects and accelerate existing projects which may contribute to the development of advanced automobile propulsion systems;
give priority attention to the development of advanced propulsion systems with appropriate attention to those advanced propulsion systems which are flexible in the type of fuel used; and
insure that research and development under this chapter supplements, but neither supplants nor duplicates, the automotive research and development efforts of private industry.

children.heading: Contracts and grants with Federal agencies, laboratories, etc.
children.label: b Contracts and grants with Federal agencies, laboratories, etc.
children.text: The Secretary of Energy shall, in fulfilling his responsibilities under this chapter, make contracts and grants with any Federal agency, laboratory, university, nonprofit organization, industrial organization, public or private agency, institution, organization, corporation, partnership, or individual for research and development leading to advanced automobile propulsion systems which are likely to help meet the Nation’s long-term goals with respect to fuel economy, environmental protection, and other objectives.

children.heading: Federal laboratories; priority for financial assistance; functions
children.label: c Federal laboratories; priority for financial assistance; functions
children.text: In providing financial assistance under this chapter, the Secretary of Energy shall give full consideration to the capabilities of Federal laboratories, except that not more than 60 per centum of the funds appropriated pursuant to the authorization under shall be directly expended in Federal laboratories. In accordance with , such laboratories shall be available for testing components and subsystems which, in the Secretary of Energy’s judgment, is likely to contribute to the development of advanced automobile propulsion systems.section 2710 of this titlesection 2706 of this title

children.heading: Evaluations, testing, information dissemination, and reporting functions
children.label: d Evaluations, testing, information dissemination, and reporting functions
children.text: The Secretary of Energy shall conduct evaluations, arrange for tests, and disseminate information pursuant to and submit reports required under .section 2706 of this titlesection 2709 of this title

children.heading: Intensification of research in basic areas by Department of Energy
children.label: e Intensification of research in basic areas by Department of Energy
children.text: The Department of Energy shall intensify research in key basic science areas in which the lack of knowledge limits development of advanced automobile propulsion systems.

children.heading: Program provisions and requirements; administrative and judicial procedures applicable to contracts, grants, or projects; additional information for reports and budget submissions; nonretro­activity of provisions and requirements
children.label: f Program provisions and requirements; administrative and judicial procedures applicable to contracts, grants, or projects; additional information for reports and budget submissions; nonretro­activity of provisions and requirements
A
children.text: supplements the automotive propulsion system research and development efforts of industry;

children.label: B
children.text: is not formulated in a manner that will supplant private industry research and development or displace or lessen industry’s research and development; and

children.label: C
children.text: avoids duplication of private research and development.

To that end, the Secretary of Energy shall issue administrative regulations, within 60 days after , which shall specify procedures, standards, and criteria for the timely review for compliance of each new contract, grant, Department of Energy project, or other agency project funded or to be funded under the authority of this Act. Such regulations shall require that the Secretary of Energy or his designee shall certify that each such contract, grant, or project satisfies the requirement of this subsection, and shall include in such certification a discussion of the relationship of any related or comparable industry research and development, in terms of this subsection, to the proposed research and development under the authority of this Act. The discussion shall also address related issues, such as cost sharing and patent rights.February 25, 1978

Such certifications shall be available to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate. The provisions of chapter 5 of title 5 shall not apply to such certifications and no court shall have any jurisdiction to review the preparation or adequacy of such certifications; but and shall apply to public disclosure of such certifications.section 553 of title 5section 5916 of title 42

The Secretary of Energy also shall include in the report required by a detailed discussion of how each research and development contract, grant, or project funded under the authority of this Act satisfies the requirement of this subsection.section 2709(a) of this title

Further, the Secretary of Energy in each annual budget submission to the Congress, or amendment thereto, for the programs authorized by this Act shall describe how each identified research and development effort in such submission satisfies the requirements of this subsection.

The provisions and requirements of this subsection shall not apply with respect to any contract, grant, or project which was entered into, made, or formally approved and initiated prior to , or with respect to any renewal or extension thereof.February 25, 1978
